architecture.summary: LLM-Geo is an autonomous GIS system that uses GPT-4 as a reasoning core to understand natural language queries about spatial problems and automatically generate code for spatial data collection, analysis, and visualization. The LLM receives a geographic question, reasons about required spatial operations, generates executable code, retrieves or processes spatial data, and returns results (numbers, graphs, maps) without human intervention.

biblio.title: Autonomous GIS: the next-generation AI-powered GIS

claims.statement: LLM-Geo returned accurate results, including aggregated numbers, graphs, and maps for all case studies
Autonomous GIS will need to achieve five autonomous goals: self-generating, self-organizing, self-verifying, self-executing, and self-growing
LLM-Geo is still in its infancy and lacks several important modules such as logging and code testing
